title: centaurium littorale subsp. uliginosum
intro: Centaurium littorale subsp. uliginosum, also known as marsh centaury, is a plant species within the gentian family that is native to wetlands and marshy areas in Europe and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, contributing to its health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, marsh centaury is often used as a tea or tincture, and it can also be found in combination with other herbs in supplements and remedies.
